title: Shadows of Brimstone - Part 2 - A Look at the Core Sets (Part One)
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rR2A9ezT_gg

Daniel (@dungeondive) cracks open the three Shadows of Brimstone core sets—City of the Ancients, Swamps of Death, and Forbidden Fortress—for a side-by-side comparison of what's inside each box.

The presentation starts with the rulebooks. The two original Western sets share the same core rulebook, while Forbidden Fortress received a cleaner, better-organised revision. Each set also includes an adventure book with setting-specific lore, missions, hero details, and enemy descriptions. Daniel notes a key disappointment: all three core sets share the same six basic missions, which he considers a significant oversight compared to Warhammer Quest's thirty.

The hero rosters get a quick overview—Swamps of Death's standout is the Preacher (Daniel's favourite archetype: the troubled holy man), while City of the Ancients features the Gunslinger with his special ammunition system. Forbidden Fortress brings the Samurai Warrior, Assassin, Traveling Monk, and Sorceress, with noticeably more interesting enemy designs including the Oni and Harionago. Daniel draws a direct line from this game to Warhammer Quest, calling Shadows of Brimstone "probably the closest thing we have to Warhammer Quest today," while noting that Flying Frog's box presentation and lore writing rival Games Workshop at their best.

----

Do you gravitate toward the Western or Eastern setting in games that offer both?
